5: From Bakunin to Lacan investigates the essential characteristics of anarchist theory, which holds that government and hierarchy are undesirable forms of social organisation.
Newman seeks to move beyond the limitations these characteristics impose on classical anarchism.
6: Philosophy of science is a branch of philosophy concerned with the foundations, methods, and implications of science.
The central questions of this study concern what qualifies as science, the reliability of scientific theories, and the ultimate purpose of science.
7: The New Age is a term applied to a range of spiritual or religious beliefs and practices that developed in Western nations during the 1970s.
Although analytically often considered to be religious, those involved in it typically prefer the designation of 'spiritual'.
8: An ontological argument is a philosophical argument for the existence of God that uses ontology.
Many arguments fall under the category of the ontological, and they tend to involve arguments about the state of being or existing.
9: Eliminative materialism is a materialist position in the philosophy of mind.
Its primary claim is that people's common-sense understanding of the mind (or folk psychology) is false.
10: The argument from morality is an argument for the existence of God.

topic.name: Australian and American Infantry Battalions
topic.description: The documents discuss the history and service of various infantry battalions from Australia and the United States, primarily during World War I and World War II.

1: The 2 / 15th Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army that served during World War II.
The battalion saw action in North Africa in 1941 – 42 as part of the 20th Brigade.
2: The 3rd Brigade, 7th Infantry Division was an infantry brigade of the United States Army.
Activated for service in World War I, the unit saw brief service in the conflict.
3: The 29th Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army.
It fought in the trenches of the Western Front in France and Belgium before being disbanded in late 1918.
4: The 27th Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army.
During World War II, the battalion was used mainly in a garrison role until the last year of the war.
5: The 2 / 31st Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army that served during World War II.
It was raised in June 1940 and was assigned to the 25th Brigade, 7th Division.
6: The 23rd Battalion was raised in Victoria in March 1915 as part of the formation of the 2nd Division of the Australian Imperial Force.
Over the course of the next two-and-a-half years, the 23rd took part in a number of significant battles in France and Belgium.
7: The 2nd Brigade, 7th Infantry Division was originally known as the 13th Infantry Brigade.
Activated for service in World War I, the unit saw brief service in the conflict.
8: The 32nd Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army.
During the Second World War the battalion was merged with the 14th Battalion to become the 14th / 32nd Battalion.
9: The 2 / 6th Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army that served during the Second World War.
The battalion formed part of the 6th Division and was among the first troops raised by Australia during the war.
10: The 26th Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army.
It was disbanded in May 1919 as part of the demobilisation of the AIF.

Its gameplay largely resembles that of traditional racing games, but also utilizes elements of kart racing and action games.

The game is set in a fictional medieval universe in which a dark wizard steals a mystical gem and captures four princesses.

The game centers on plumbers Mario and Luigi who embark on a quest to save Princess Toadstool.

The gameplay focuses on Mario's extermination of them by flipping them on their backs and kicking them away.

Players control Wonder Boy through twelve linear levels as he makes his way through Monster Land to find and defeat the MEKA dragon.

The game's objective is to destroy the viruses populating the on-screen playing field by using colored capsules.
8: Marvel vs. Capcom 2 is a crossover fighting game developed and published by Capcom.
Players select a team of characters from the Marvel and Capcom universes to engage in combat.
9: Mario Kart DS is a go-kart racing game developed and published by Nintendo.

10: Naruto: Clash of Ninja is a 3-D cel-shaded fighting game developed by Eighting.

topic.name: Political Campaigns and Elections

Baldwin expressed paleo-conservative positions on a number of issues including protectionism on trade, nonintervention in foreign affairs.

He ran on a platform that included non-interventionism in foreign conflicts, decriminalization of illegal drugs on a federal level.
Document 4: The New Zealand Labour Party leadership election, 2011 was held on 13 December 2011 to choose the thirteenth leader of the New Zealand Labour Party.
Following the Labour Party's loss in the 2011 general election, leader Phil Goff and deputy leader Annette King resigned.
Document 5: The 2014 Vermont gubernatorial election took place on November 4, 2014, to elect the Governor of Vermont.

Following the resignations of Johann Lamont as leader and Anas Sarwar as deputy.

topic.name: Pulp Science Fiction Magazines
topic.description: The documents discuss various pulp science fiction magazines published primarily in the mid-20th century, focusing on their publication history, content, and the changes in the science fiction genre during that era.

The early 1950s saw dramatic changes in the world of U.S. science fiction publishing.
2: Fantastic Story Quarterly was a pulp science fiction magazine, published from 1950 to 1955.
It was launched to reprint stories from the early years of the science fiction pulp magazines.
3: Ghost Stories was a pulp magazine which published 64 issues between 1926 and 1932.
It was one of the earliest competitors to Weird Tales.
4: Dynamic Science Fiction was a pulp magazine which published six issues from December 1952 to January 1954.
It was launched at the end of the pulp era.
5: Wonder Story Annual was a science fiction pulp magazine which was launched in 1950.
It was created as a vehicle to reprint stories from early issues of Wonder Stories.
6: Dynamic Science Stories was a pulp magazine which published two issues, dated February and April 1939.
It was a companion to Marvel Science Stories.
7: A. Merritt's Fantasy Magazine was a pulp magazine which published five issues from December 1949 to October 1950.
It aimed to capitalize on Merritt's popularity.
8: Strange Stories was a pulp magazine which ran for thirteen issues from 1939 to 1941.
It was edited by Mort Weisinger.
9: Two Complete Science-Adventure Books was a pulp science fiction magazine, published by Fiction House, which lasted for eleven issues between 1950 and 1954.
The early 1950s saw dramatic changes in the world of U.S. science fiction publishing.
10: Out of This World Adventures was a pulp magazine which published two issues, in July and December 1950.
It included several pages of comics as well as science fiction stories.

topic.name: Liquid Crystals and Their Properties
topic.description: The documents discuss the nature, classification, and characteristics of liquid crystals, including their phases and optical properties.

Document 1: Liquid crystals ( LCs ) are matter in a state which has properties between those of conventional liquids and those of solid crystals.
Liquid crystals can be divided into thermotropic, lyotropic and metallotropic phases.
Document 2: Liquid crystals ( LCs ) are matter in a state which has properties between those of conventional liquids and those of solid crystals.
There are many different types of liquid-crystal phases, which can be distinguished by their different optical properties.

topic.name: Ethnic Conflict and Nationalism
topic.description: The documents discuss various instances of ethnic conflict and the rise of nationalism in different regions, particularly during and after colonial rule.

1: International Gendarmerie was established to maintain order during Albania's transition to independence.
The gendarmerie faced challenges including a peasant revolt shortly after its formation.
2: The Rwandan Revolution was marked by ethnic violence between Hutu and Tutsi groups.
The revolution transitioned Rwanda from a Tutsi monarchy to a Hutu-dominated republic.
3: PADESM was formed in Madagascar to empower coastal peoples against Merina dominance.
Political tensions led to violence between rival nationalist groups during the Malagasy Uprising.
4: The Congo Crisis involved civil wars and ethnic tensions following independence from Belgium.
Nationalist movements and external powers influenced the conflict dynamics.
5: Shukri al-Quwatli was a key figure in Syria's independence movement amidst regional tensions.
His political career reflects the struggles of nationalist movements in the Arab world.
6: South Kasai declared secession during the Congo Crisis, highlighting ethnic divisions.
The region's leaders exploited ethnic tensions to establish a Luba-focused state.
7: Mladen Lorković's political actions were influenced by the nationalist sentiments of his time.
His involvement in the Ustaše reflects the complexities of ethnic nationalism during WWII.
8: The German Eastern Marches Society aimed to promote Germanization and suppress Polish identity.
It was part of the broader nationalist movements in Eastern Europe during the Wilhelmine era.
9: German nationalism in Austria sought to unify German-speaking populations under a Greater Germany.
It played a significant role in Austria's political landscape throughout its history.
10: The Polish Underground State was a response to occupation, reflecting nationalist sentiments during WWII.
It encompassed both military and civilian resistance against foreign powers.

topic.name: Atlantic Hurricane Seasons
topic.description: A comprehensive overview of various Atlantic hurricane seasons, detailing the formation, impact, and statistics of tropical cyclones from different years.

1: The 2001 Atlantic hurricane season was a fairly active Atlantic hurricane season that produced 17 tropical cyclones, 15 named storms, nine hurricanes, and four major hurricanes.
The most intense storm was Hurricane Michelle, which attained Category 4 strength on the Saffir – Simpson Hurricane Scale.
2: The 2004 Atlantic hurricane season was the costliest Atlantic hurricane season on record until surpassed by the following year.
Hurricane Charley made landfall in Florida as a Category 4 hurricane on the Saffir – Simpson hurricane wind scale (SSHWS), causing $15.1 billion in damage in the United States alone.
3: The 1900 Atlantic hurricane season featured seven known tropical cyclones, three of which made landfall in the United States.
Nicknamed the 'Galveston hurricane of 1900', it remains the deadliest natural disaster in the history of the United States.
4: The 1965 Atlantic hurricane season was the first to use the modern-day bounds for an Atlantic hurricane season, which are June 1 to November 30.
Hurricane Betsy was the strongest and most devastating storm of the season.
5: The 1989 Atlantic hurricane season featured the costliest tropical cyclone in the Atlantic basin at the time, Hurricane Hugo.
Hurricane Hugo, a Category 5 hurricane, caused at least $10 billion in damage and 88 fatalities.
6: The 1968 Atlantic hurricane season was one of five Atlantic hurricane seasons during the satellite era not to feature a major hurricane.
Hurricane Abby developed in the northwestern Caribbean Sea on June 1.
7: The 1980 Atlantic hurricane season was tied with 1932, 1969, and 1994 for most named storms in Atlantic Ocean during the month of November.
Hurricane Allen was then the earliest Category 5 hurricane on record and also devastated portions of the Caribbean Sea.
